findings.summary: infrastructure/core/cli.py imports discover_projects from infrastructure/project inside function bodies at two call sites, inverting the dependency direction
evidence: cli.py: from infrastructure.project.discovery import discover_projects inside handle_multi_project_command()
cli.py: from infrastructure.project.discovery import discover_projects inside handle_discover_command()
multi_project.py TYPE_CHECKING guard is the correct mitigation but cli.py has two unguarded runtime imports from project into core
findings.suggestion: Accept a project-discovery callable as a parameter to main() or handle_multi_project_command() rather than importing inside the function body.

findings.summary: infrastructure/validation/doc_discovery.py has a top-level import from infrastructure.project.discovery creating compile-time cross-package coupling
evidence: doc_discovery.py: from infrastructure.project.discovery import discover_projects at module top level
output_validator.py: same import deferred inside function body — inconsistent mitigation pattern
findings.suggestion: Pass project_names: set[str] as a parameter to find_markdown_files and validate_root_output_structure to remove the cross-package dependency.

findings.summary: Convention scanner flags 52 infrastructure modules as missing get_logger/Path but most are __init__.py or leaf files where omission is appropriate
evidence: 52 infrastructure files flagged as outliers for missing get_logger or Path
infrastructure/llm/review/generator.py flagged as missing get_logger but logger = get_logger(__name__) is present — false positive
findings.suggestion: Update the convention scanner exclusion list to skip __init__.py, constants, and model-only files from the get_logger/Path requirement.

findings.summary: Multiple pipeline modules catch broad Exception without wrapping or classifying, losing error context at module boundaries
evidence: pipeline.py: multiple broad except Exception handlers that log and return without re-raising or wrapping with context
Callers cannot distinguish infrastructure failure from business logic failure from the exception type alone
findings.suggestion: Narrow exception catches to specific types or wrap broad catches in domain-specific exception types (e.g., PipelineError) before re-raising, preserving the original cause as __cause__.

findings.summary: requests library is a heavy dependency used solely for Ollama HTTP calls where httpx or stdlib urllib would suffice
evidence: client.py imports requests for POST calls to the Ollama API at localhost
requests is listed in pyproject.toml main dependencies despite being used in only one module for local HTTP
findings.suggestion: Consider replacing requests with httpx (already available via other deps) or stdlib urllib.request for the single Ollama HTTP use case to reduce the dependency surface.

findings.summary: dashboard_generator.py has 192 indirection chains at depth 3, the highest in the codebase, from mixing domain logic with matplotlib API traversal
evidence: dashboard_generator.py max_chain_depth=3, chain_count=192
Module mixes matplotlib API calls with ProjectMetrics aggregation in the same functions
findings.suggestion: Extract data preparation into pure functions separate from matplotlib rendering calls.

findings.summary: steganography/overlays.py has 2 wide-parameter functions inconsistent with the config-object pattern in sibling steganography/core.py
evidence: steganography/overlays.py wide_functions=2, config_bag_mentions=0
steganography/core.py uses config_bag_mentions=37 — sibling is inconsistent
findings.suggestion: Define an OverlayConfig dataclass mirroring the existing steganography config pattern.

findings.summary: Several infrastructure modules retain verbose Args/Returns docstrings on functions whose signatures are already self-documenting via type annotations
evidence: environment.py: multiple functions with Args/Returns sections that restate parameter names and type hints verbatim
multi_project_reporter.py: generate_multi_project_report has a 10-line docstring enumerating behavior already clear from the function body
findings.suggestion: Keep the one-line summary only on functions where type annotations and parameter names are already unambiguous. Reserve Args/Returns for non-obvious contracts.

findings.summary: projects/code_project/scripts/optimization_analysis.py is 1654 LOC with zero importers and no test coverage
evidence: Untested critical module (1654 LOC, 0 importers) — high blast radius
Contains 2x sys.path mutation at import time, 2x monster functions >150 LOC
findings.suggestion: Extract domain logic into projects/code_project/src/ so it can be unit-tested.

findings.summary: scripts/01_run_tests.py is 623 LOC with zero importers and no test coverage despite being core pipeline infrastructure
evidence: 623 LOC orchestrator with zero importers — untested core infrastructure
Module imports from infrastructure but is itself never imported or tested
findings.suggestion: Extract logic-heavy portions into infrastructure/reporting/ so they can be tested independently.

findings.summary: infrastructure/reporting/test_runner.py belongs conceptually in infrastructure/core/ not reporting/
evidence: test_runner.py provides pytest subprocess execution and retry/coverage loop logic
All other reporting/ files generate output artifacts; test_runner.py drives process execution
findings.suggestion: Move test_runner.py to infrastructure/core/ alongside pipeline.py and checkpoint.py.

findings.summary: The logging concern is split across 5 files in infrastructure/core/ with no satellite file having independent importers
evidence: logging_utils.py is the hub (135 importers) and re-imports from 4 satellite files
None of the satellite files have independent importers
findings.suggestion: Collapse the 4 satellite files into logging_utils.py or a single logging/ subpackage.

findings.summary: AccuracyIssue, CompletenessGap, and ScanResults are defined in both repo_scanner.py and doc_models.py
evidence: repo_scanner.py defines AccuracyIssue, CompletenessGap, ScanResults as dataclasses
doc_scanner.py imports and re-exports the same names from doc_models.py
Two ScanResults types have different fields creating caller ambiguity
findings.suggestion: Consolidate into one location. Rename to disambiguate if semantics differ (e.g., RepoScanResults vs DocScanResults).

findings.summary: PromptComposer.compose_template contains hardcoded test-specific fragment detection and default headers in production code
evidence: Literal test fragment name #test_template baked into production dispatch
Default test headers embedded as production fallback: headers = ['## Section1', '## Section2']  # Default for tests
findings.suggestion: Replace ad-hoc string-matching fragment dispatch with a registry or enum. Remove #test_template special case from production code.

findings.summary: get_manuscript_review_system_prompt() duplicates a long system prompt that also exists in the prompt loader
evidence: 15-line literal system prompt string is the hard-coded fallback when PROMPT_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE is False
Same content loaded via loader.get_system_prompt when available — two separately maintained versions
findings.suggestion: Make the prompt file the single source. Raise ConfigurationError if missing rather than silently falling back.

findings.summary: generate_multi_project_summary_report computes proj_dur twice by iterating summary[projects] twice for slowest/fastest and for total
evidence: Lines 121-131: durations list built then proj_dur list rebuilt from the same summary[projects] dict in the same try block
Both loops iterate the same data with slightly different conditions — could be a single pass
findings.suggestion: Compute both durations and proj_dur in a single iteration over summary[projects].items() to eliminate the redundant pass.

findings.summary: PipelineSummaryGenerator has three near-identical format methods each re-walking the same summary fields
evidence: All three format methods iterate stage_results, compute executed_stages, call _find_slowest/fastest independently
Any new summary field must be added in all three methods
findings.suggestion: Extract a summary-to-dict normalizer called once, then pass its output to thin format-specific renderers.
